summary: This brief post announces a new Bloomberg column examining the reliability of Covid-19 data. O'Neil argues there are at least ten reasons to question the accuracy of the pandemic statistics being reported daily. She notes that delayed data from Easter weekend will likely compound existing data quality issues. The post suggests that the true impact of the pandemic may never be accurately measured due to systemic data collection problems.
key_points: Covid-19 data reporting has significant reliability issues that warrant skepticism
Holiday weekends like Easter create additional delays and distortions in the data
The pandemic's actual toll may remain permanently unknowable due to data limitations
money_quotes: The pandemic's true toll might never be known.
key_insight: The daily Covid-19 statistics we rely on for understanding the pandemic are fundamentally unreliable, and we may never know the true scope of its impact.
